title: Ageratina caeciliae
intro: Ageratina caeciliae, also known as Eupatoria, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, asthma, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina caeciliae include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ageratina caeciliae is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, pain relief, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Ageratina caeciliae is classified in the kingdom Plantae, further divided into the clade Angiosperms, followed by the clade Eudicots, and then the order Asterales. Within the order Asterales, it belongs to the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Ageratina is a group of flowering plants, and Ageratina caeciliae is a specific species within this genus. It is a member of the Ageratina genus, which is often associated with the mint family, Lamiaceae.
